methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $93M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $10.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Chai Discovery

data.narrative:
Chemify is a Series B biotech company founded by Leroy Cronin that has raised $93M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private companies and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $93M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $177M. Peer-median analysis examines four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Colossal Biosciences ($10.3B Series C), Chai Discovery ($3.8B Series B), New Limit ($3.1B Series C), and Eikon Therapeutics ($539M Series B). The median across these four peers is $3.5B, with a range of $539M to $10.3B. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable because Chemify's revenue is not disclosed, preventing P/S multiple application despite a 6.9x sector median across three public biotech tickers.

The two applicable methods produce a wide range. Stage-progression implies $177M; peer-median implies $3.5B. The triangulated valuation range is $150M (low) to $4.0B (high), with a median of $1.8B. Key uncertainties driving this dispersion include stage-of-maturity relative to the peer set—Chemify is Series B while half the comparables are Series C—and the significant spread in peer valuations, reflecting heterogeneity in technology readiness and commercial progress within frontier biotech.
